title: Some Effective Special Point Results
speaker: Gareth Jones
abstract: I'll start by discussing some work with Binyamini, Schmidt, and Thomas in which we prove a uniform Manin-Mumford result for products of CM elliptic curves. I'll show how we apply this to obtain an effective Andre-Oort result for fibre powers of the Legendre family. I'll then discuss work in progress with Schmidt, on extending this to multiplicative extensions.
tags: Workshop on Recent Developments in Hodge Theory and O-minimality

proof_stages.label: Manin-Mumford as the torsion-point model
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins with Raynaud's Manin-Mumford theorem, interpreting torsion points on a subvariety of an abelian variety as accounted for by finitely many torsion cosets and motivating effective and uniform versions.

proof_stages.label: Uniform effective Manin-Mumford for CM elliptic products
proof_stages.summary: The speaker states joint work giving effective constants that bound the order of torsion points on subvarieties of products of CM elliptic curves, under the absence of positive-dimensional torsion cosets, in terms of controlled field and degree data.

proof_stages.label: Effective Pila-Zannier counting input
proof_stages.summary: The proof follows the Pila-Zannier strategy by passing special or torsion points to rational points in a definable setting, applying effective Pila-Wilkie counting for restricted Pfaffian/o-minimal functions, and using uniform definability of the relevant Weierstrass elliptic functions.

proof_stages.label: CM Galois lower bounds and their limitations
proof_stages.summary: The arithmetic half of the argument supplies Galois-orbit lower bounds for torsion points in CM products, explains where the CM hypothesis enters, and identifies the non-CM and quadratic-discriminant cases as the main obstructions to stronger uniformity.

proof_stages.label: Legendre-family special-point theorem
proof_stages.summary: The talk applies the CM Manin-Mumford result to the g-fold fiber power of the Legendre family, viewed as a mixed Shimura variety, defines special points via torsion in CM fibers, recalls the qualitative special-point theorem, and states an effective bound for the resulting point complexity.

proof_stages.label: Fiberwise reduction and discriminant bound
proof_stages.summary: For a special point, the speaker intersects the variety with its CM fiber, applies the effective CM Manin-Mumford bound to control the torsion order, then uses effective Galois bounds to control the CM discriminant and thereby obtains an effective André-Oort-type reduction.

proof_stages.label: Work in progress on semi-abelian extensions
proof_stages.summary: The final section proposes analogous effective special-point bounds for extensions of products of elliptic curves by powers of the multiplicative group, first over a fixed CM base and then in a larger mixed Shimura family, with ongoing work aimed at removing positive-dimensional-special assumptions.

examples.name: Non-CM Legendre specialization with a fixed torsion point
examples.note: A Legendre elliptic curve with a point such as one of fixed x-coordinate specializes at infinitely many parameters where that point becomes torsion of unbounded order, showing why the CM hypothesis is needed for the stated uniform degree-based bound.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker constructs the Legendre-family specialization with a fixed point, notes its degree over the parameter field is 2, and uses it to demonstrate unbounded torsion orders without the CM assumption
